rag_rules: In implementation contracts, only use assign for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables; avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope.
When AGREE reports type errors or unresolved references, trace the root cause to invalid helper declarations or missing identifiers on the same line and fix or remove the offending clause.
generic_rules: Ensure all identifiers referenced in AGREE contracts are visible in the current scope and properly declared.
Use only valid AADL features or declared helpers within the same annex to avoid type errors.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation-level scopes; use component type scopes for such contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must have consistent types; avoid mixing <error> types with valid ones.
Assign statements in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared variables.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to resolve type errors; instead, fix or remove invalid clauses.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation-level scopes where only assign and output features are valid.
